description: Refactor backlog_scanner.py to align with idiomatic Python 3.13 patterns and best practices — modern type hints, custom exceptions, EAFP, dataclass improvements, generators, enums, protocols, and tooling config

userStories.title: Modernize type hints to Python 3.13 style
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the codebase to use modern Python 3.13 type hints so the code is cleaner and doesn't rely on legacy typing imports.

userStories.title: Add custom exception hierarchy
userStories.description: As a developer, I want specific exception types so error handling is explicit and debuggable rather than catching broad OSError everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `ConfigError(ScannerError)` for invalid CLI arguments or configuration
Replace bare `OSError` catches with specific exception handling and chaining (`raise ... from e`)
`main()` catches `ScannerError` and exits cleanly with error message

userStories.title: Apply EAFP pattern and improve error handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the code to follow Python's EAFP idiom so exception handling is idiomatic and consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: `relative_to()` calls use try/except instead of pre-checking
File reading uses EAFP with specific exception types
Exception chaining (`from e`) used wherever exceptions are re-raised
No silent exception swallowing (no bare `except: pass`)

userStories.title: Improve dataclass usage and add validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want dataclasses to use __post_init__ validation and __slots__ for memory efficiency so the data model is robust and performant.
acceptanceCriteria: Use `frozen=True` on `TodoItem` since items shouldn't be mutated after creation

userStories.title: Use generators for lazy evaluation in large scans
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the scanner to use generators and lazy evaluation so it handles large repositories without excessive memory usage.
acceptanceCriteria: `scan_file` returns an `Iterator[TodoItem]` instead of building a full list
`generate_backlog_md` uses `StringIO` or generator-based string building instead of list concatenation
String building avoids `+=` in loops (use `join()` or `StringIO`)

userStories.title: Extract constants and use enums for marker types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want marker types and scoring constants defined as enums/constants so the scoring logic is self-documenting and easy to modify.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract impact keyword mappings into a structured constant (dict or enum)
Extract effort thresholds into named constants (`EFFORT_SHORT = 50`, `EFFORT_LONG = 150`)
Extract priority clamping bounds into constants (`MIN_PRIORITY = 1`, `MAX_PRIORITY = 10`)

userStories.title: Improve function signatures and use Protocol for extensibility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clean function signatures and protocol-based abstractions so the code is extensible and self-documenting.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a `ModuleDetector` protocol: `def detect(file_path: Path, root: Path) -> str | None`
Register detectors by file extension instead of if/elif chain in `detect_module`
Module detection functions follow a consistent Protocol or callable signature

userStories.title: Add pyproject.toml with full tooling configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a pyproject.toml with formatter, linter, type checker, and test runner configuration so the project has consistent development standards.
acceptanceCriteria: `pyproject.toml` created with project metadata (name, version, requires-python >= 3.13)
`[tool.ruff]` configured with select rules: E, F, I, N, W
`[tool.pytest.ini_options]` configured with testpaths and coverage options
Dev dependencies listed: pytest, pytest-cov, black, ruff, mypy

userStories.title: Apply naming and style conventions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want consistent naming and style conventions so the code reads naturally and follows PEP 8.
acceptanceCriteria: Import order follows: stdlib then third-party then local (isort via ruff)
`isinstance()` used instead of `type()` for type checks
